---
title: "fasterslower_context_glmms"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-24"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(emmeans)
library(ggplot2)
library(glmmTMB)
library(effects)
library(car)
library(DHARMa)
library(performance)
library(effectsize)
```

```{r}
ctx <- read_csv("C:/Users/Marcel/Desktop/Study Project/VR Data/ctx_fasterslower.csv")

ctx <- ctx %>%
  mutate(
    SubjectID   = factor(SubjectID),
    Residual   = factor(Residual, levels = c("Faster","Slower")),
    AmpBin      = factor(AmpBin, levels = sort(unique(AmpBin))),    
    within_object   = as.numeric(within_object),
    category_switch = as.numeric(category_switch),
    next_is_novel   = as.numeric(next_is_novel),
    prev_is_novel   = as.numeric(prev_is_novel)
  )

ctx <- ctx |>
  mutate(
    Faster01  = as.integer(Residual == "Faster")
  )
  
str(ctx)

# Calculate group means per cell (Faster/Slower within AmpBins) since glmms on these cell means are more normally distributed than running the glmm's with the continuous dependent variables on a saccade level.
ctx_cell <- ctx %>%
  group_by(SubjectID, AmpBin, Residual) %>%
  summarise(
    # total saccades in this cell
    n_sacc = n(),

    # ----- continuous context: cell means -----
    prev_fix_ms      = mean(prev_fix_ms,      na.rm = TRUE),
    post_fix_ms      = mean(post_fix_ms,      na.rm = TRUE),
    prev_sacc_amp_deg = mean(prev_sacc_amp_deg, na.rm = TRUE),
    prev_sacc_dur_ms  = mean(prev_sacc_dur_ms,  na.rm = TRUE),
    next_sacc_amp_deg = mean(next_sacc_amp_deg, na.rm = TRUE),
    next_sacc_dur_ms  = mean(next_sacc_dur_ms,  na.rm = TRUE),
    prev_dist        = mean(prev_dist,        na.rm = TRUE),
    next_dist        = mean(next_dist,        na.rm = TRUE),
    head_pre         = mean(head_pre,         na.rm = TRUE),
    head_post        = mean(head_post,        na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() 

str(ctx_cell)

set.seed(1)

ctx$AmpBin_num <- as.numeric(ctx$AmpBin)
```

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# run_context_glmm
#
# Fits a binomial GLMM:
#   context_var ~ AmpBin * Residual + (1 | SubjectID)
#
# In addition to the model and plot, this function also:
#   - computes Fast vs Slow contrasts within each amplitude bin
#   - applies multiplicity correction ACROSS the full set of bin-wise contrasts
#     via cross.adjust in summary()
#   - prints both the full contrast table and the significant contrasts
#
# Note:
#   Using adjust = "holm" directly inside contrast() is not sufficient here,
#   because there is only one Fast-vs-Slow contrast per AmpBin. To adjust
#   across all amplitude bins, the correction must be applied across the set
#   of by-group contrasts via cross.adjust.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
run_context_glmm <- function(
  ctx,
  context_var,
  context_label,
  y_limit = c(0, 1),
  legend_position = "bottom",
  contrast_adjust = "holm"
) {
  form_str <- paste0(context_var, " ~ AmpBin * Residual + (1 | SubjectID)")

  m <- glmmTMB(
    as.formula(form_str),
    data = ctx,
    family = binomial(link = "logit"),
    control = glmmTMBControl(
      optimizer = optim,
      optArgs = list(method = "BFGS")
    )
  )

  res <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = m, n = 1000, refit = FALSE)
  plot(res)

  cat("\n=== GLMM for", context_var, "~ AmpBin * Residual ===\n")
  print(summary(m))
  print(Anova(m))

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Estimated marginal means for plotting on the response scale
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  em_plot <- emmeans(m, ~ AmpBin * Residual, type = "response")
  df_em <- as.data.frame(confint(em_plot, level = 0.83))

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Fast vs Slow contrasts within each amplitude bin
  #
  # Important:
  #   We first compute the pairwise contrasts without adjustment inside
  #   contrast(). Then we apply the multiple-comparison correction across the
  #   full set of AmpBin-specific contrasts using cross.adjust in summary().
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  em_contr <- emmeans(m, ~ Residual | AmpBin, type = "response")

  bin_contrasts <- contrast(
    em_contr,
    method = "pairwise"
  )

  bin_contrasts_df <- as.data.frame(
    summary(
      bin_contrasts,
      infer = c(TRUE, TRUE),
      adjust = "none",
      cross.adjust = contrast_adjust
    )
  )

  sig_bin_contrasts_df <- bin_contrasts_df %>%
    dplyr::filter(p.value < 0.05)

  cat("\n=== Fast vs Slow contrasts within AmpBin for", context_var, "===\n")
  print(bin_contrasts_df)

  cat("\n=== Significant Fast vs Slow contrasts within AmpBin for", context_var, "===\n")
  print(sig_bin_contrasts_df)

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Plot
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  p <- ggplot(
    df_em,
    aes(
      x = AmpBin,
      y = prob,
      color = Residual,
      group = Residual,
      fill = Residual
    )
  ) +
    geom_line(
      linewidth = 2.5,
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.35)
    ) +
    geom_point(
      aes(shape = Residual),
      size = 5,
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.35)
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      aes(ymin = asymp.LCL, ymax = asymp.UCL),
      alpha = 0.30,
      colour = NA,
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.35)
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      limits = y_limit,
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.04))
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(Faster = "springgreen4", Slower = "darkgoldenrod2"),
      labels = c(
        Faster = "Fast",
        Slower = "Slow"
      )
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(Faster = "springgreen4", Slower = "darkgoldenrod2"),
      labels = c(
        Faster = "Fast",
        Slower = "Slow"
      )
    ) +
    scale_shape_manual(
      values = c(Faster = 16, Slower = 17),
      labels = c(
        Faster = "Fast",
        Slower = "Slow"
      )
    ) +
    labs(
      subtitle = paste0("Model-estimated P(", context_label, ") by amplitude bin"),
      color = "Residual",
      fill = "Residual",
      shape = "Residual",
      x = NULL,
      y = "Probability (model-based, logit GLMM)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      text = element_text(size = 20),
      legend.position = legend_position,
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 26),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26),
      axis.text.x = element_text(
        size = 22,
        face = "bold",
        angle = 45,
        hjust = 1,
        vjust = 1
      ),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  print(p)

  invisible(list(
    model = m,
    residuals = res,
    emmeans_plot = em_plot,
    em_df = df_em,
    emmeans_contrasts = em_contr,
    contrasts = bin_contrasts,
    contrasts_df = bin_contrasts_df,
    significant_contrasts_df = sig_bin_contrasts_df,
    plot = p
  ))
}


# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# run_continuous_context_lmm
#
# Fits a Gaussian mixed model on subject × residual × amplitude-bin cell means:
#   outcome_var ~ AmpBin * Residual + (1 | SubjectID)
#
# In addition to the model and plot, this function also:
#   - computes Fast vs Slow contrasts within each amplitude bin
#   - applies multiplicity correction ACROSS the full set of bin-wise contrasts
#     via cross.adjust in summary()
#   - prints both the full contrast table and the significant contrasts
#
# Note:
#   As above, using adjust = "holm" directly inside contrast() is not sufficient
#   for correction across amplitude bins. The adjustment must be applied across
#   the set of by-group contrasts via cross.adjust.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
run_continuous_context_lmm <- function(
  ctx_cell,
  outcome_var,
  outcome_label,
  y_label = NULL,
  legend_position = "bottom",
  contrast_adjust = "holm"
) {
  if (is.null(y_label)) {
    y_label <- outcome_label
  }

  form_str <- paste0(outcome_var, " ~ AmpBin * Residual + (1 | SubjectID)")

  m <- glmmTMB(
    as.formula(form_str),
    data = ctx_cell,
    family = gaussian(link = "identity"),
    control = glmmTMBControl(
      optimizer = optim,
      optArgs = list(method = "BFGS")
    )
  )

  res <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = m, n = 1000, refit = FALSE)
  plot(res)

  cat("\n=== Gaussian GLMM (cell level) for", outcome_var, "~ AmpBin * Residual ===\n")
  print(summary(m))
  print(Anova(m))

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Estimated marginal means for plotting
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  em_plot <- emmeans(m, ~ AmpBin * Residual)
  df_em <- as.data.frame(confint(em_plot, level = 0.83))

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Fast vs Slow contrasts within each amplitude bin
  #
  # Important:
  #   We first compute the pairwise contrasts without adjustment inside
  #   contrast(). Then we apply the multiple-comparison correction across the
  #   full set of AmpBin-specific contrasts using cross.adjust in summary().
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  em_contr <- emmeans(m, ~ Residual | AmpBin)

  bin_contrasts <- contrast(
    em_contr,
    method = "pairwise"
  )

  bin_contrasts_df <- as.data.frame(
    summary(
      bin_contrasts,
      infer = c(TRUE, TRUE),
      adjust = "none",
      cross.adjust = contrast_adjust
    )
  )

  sig_bin_contrasts_df <- bin_contrasts_df %>%
    dplyr::filter(p.value < 0.05)

  cat("\n=== Fast vs Slow contrasts within AmpBin for", outcome_var, "===\n")
  print(bin_contrasts_df)

  cat("\n=== Significant Fast vs Slow contrasts within AmpBin for", outcome_var, "===\n")
  print(sig_bin_contrasts_df)

  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Plot
  # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  p <- ggplot(
    df_em,
    aes(
      x = AmpBin,
      y = emmean,
      color = Residual,
      group = Residual,
      fill = Residual
    )
  ) +
    geom_line(
      linewidth = 2.5,
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.35)
    ) +
    geom_point(
      aes(shape = Residual),
      size = 5,
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.35)
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      aes(ymin = lower.CL, ymax = upper.CL),
      alpha = 0.30,
      colour = NA,
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.35)
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(Faster = "springgreen4", Slower = "darkgoldenrod2"),
      labels = c(
        Faster = "Fast",
        Slower = "Slow"
      )
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(Faster = "springgreen4", Slower = "darkgoldenrod2"),
      labels = c(
        Faster = "Fast",
        Slower = "Slow"
      )
    ) +
    scale_shape_manual(
      values = c(Faster = 16, Slower = 17),
      labels = c(
        Faster = "Fast",
        Slower = "Slow"
      )
    ) +
    labs(
      subtitle = paste0("Estimated mean ", outcome_label, " by amplitude bin"),
      color = "Residual",
      fill = "Residual",
      shape = "Residual",
      x = NULL,
      y = y_label
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      text = element_text(size = 20),
      legend.position = legend_position,
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 26),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26),
      axis.text.x = element_text(
        size = 22,
        face = "bold",
        angle = 45,
        hjust = 1,
        vjust = 1
      ),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  print(p)

  invisible(list(
    model = m,
    residuals = res,
    emmeans_plot = em_plot,
    em_df = df_em,
    emmeans_contrasts = em_contr,
    contrasts = bin_contrasts,
    contrasts_df = bin_contrasts_df,
    significant_contrasts_df = sig_bin_contrasts_df,
    plot = p
  ))
}
```

```{r}
res_within <- run_context_glmm(
  ctx,
  context_var    = "within_object",
  context_label  = "within object",
  y_limit        = c(0, 0.9)
)
```
```{r}
res_switch <- run_context_glmm(
  ctx,
  context_var    = "category_switch",
  context_label  = "category switch",
  y_limit        = c(0, 0.6)
)
```

```{r}
res_nextnovel <- run_context_glmm(
  ctx,
  context_var    = "next_is_novel",
  context_label  = "next is novel",
  y_limit        = c(0, 0.3)
)

```

```{r}
res_prevnovel <- run_context_glmm(
  ctx,
  context_var    = "prev_is_novel",
  context_label  = "previous fixation is novel",
  y_limit        = c(0, 0.3)
)
```







Continuous Dependent Variables:
```{r}
# prev_fix_ms as outcome
res_prev_fix <- run_continuous_context_lmm(
  ctx_cell,
  outcome_var   = "prev_fix_ms",
  outcome_label = "Previous fixation duration (ms)"
)
```

```{r}
# post_fix_ms as outcome
res_post_fix <- run_continuous_context_lmm(
  ctx_cell,
  outcome_var   = "post_fix_ms",
  outcome_label = "Subsequent fixation duration (ms)"
)

```



```{r}
# prev_dist as outcome
res_prev_dist <- run_continuous_context_lmm(
  ctx_cell,
  outcome_var   = "prev_dist",
  outcome_label = "Previous distance (m)"
)
```

```{r}
# next_dist as outcome
res_next_dist <- run_continuous_context_lmm(
  ctx_cell,
  outcome_var   = "next_dist",
  outcome_label = "Next distance (m)"
)
```

```{r}
# head_pre as outcome
res_head_pre <- run_continuous_context_lmm(
  ctx_cell,
  outcome_var   = "head_pre",
  outcome_label = "Head velocity pre (deg/s)"
)
```

```{r}
# head_post as outcome
res_head_post <- run_continuous_context_lmm(
  ctx_cell,
  outcome_var   = "head_post",
  outcome_label = "Head velocity post (deg/s)"
)
```

```{r}
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Save contextual model plots
# ---------------------------------------------------------
plot_dir <- "C:/Users/Marcel/Desktop/Study Project/VR Data/Plots"

# Create folder if it does not exist
if (!dir.exists(plot_dir)) {
  dir.create(plot_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

# Collect plots in a named list
plot_list <- list(
  within_object   = res_within$plot,
  category_switch = res_switch$plot,
  next_is_novel   = res_nextnovel$plot,
  prev_is_novel   = res_prevnovel$plot,
  prev_fix_ms     = res_prev_fix$plot,
  post_fix_ms     = res_post_fix$plot,
  prev_dist       = res_prev_dist$plot,
  next_dist       = res_next_dist$plot,
  head_pre        = res_head_pre$plot,
  head_post       = res_head_post$plot
)

# Save each plot
for (plot_name in names(plot_list)) {
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(plot_dir, paste0(plot_name, ".png")),
    plot     = plot_list[[plot_name]],
    width    = 12,
    height   = 8,
    dpi      = 300
  )
}
```
